name: Shutdown of wind turbines
description: State F's wind farm has suddenly shut down. Hackers are known to have gained access to the control system of the wind turbines and disabled them. A deal with an investment company that planned to invest in the project now is in jeopardy. Partners are wondering: "Are you really that easy to hack?"
technicalDescription: Gain access to the WindTurbines operator's workstation. Stop all wind turbines — change the maximum allowed wind speed to 0.

name: Gaining control over digital ruble operations at First Partner Bank
description:
First Partner Bank is investigating a possible compromise of the administrative segment of its digital ruble platform. According to an internal audit, a new user account with Information Security Administrator privileges appeared in the system.

The issue goes far beyond the creation of a rogue account. A user with this level of access can manage security policies, review transactions, modify monitoring settings, and interfere with transaction-processing workflows.

In the banking sector, such systems are considered critically important because they handle operations for businesses, government agencies, and private customers. Any unauthorized role modification becomes a threat to the entire financial ecosystem.

“If attackers truly obtained administrative privileges, this is no longer just about stealing money — it is about the ability to manipulate financial processes without being noticed,” a regulator representative stated.
technicalDescription: Create a new user for the digital ruble workstation. Gain access to digital ruble operations management in First Partner Bank with the "Information Security Administrator" role.

name: Shutdown of CHP steam turbine
description:
This afternoon, hackers attacked a CHP steam turbine in State F. Hundreds of settlements nationwide were left without power.

Since the beginning of the heating season, several dozen fake domains have appeared on the internet, echoing the names of state energy companies, in particular F-Gas and F-Power. The likely objective was to harvest data on employees in the energy sector. This information was then used for attacks on industrial facilities.

Hackers were able to break into the CHP control system and stop the steam turbine. As a result, parts of State F were left without power. This brought transport, government, and commercial services to a halt. Malls and retail stores were forced to close in the middle of the working day because without cash registers there can be no trade.

According to government estimates, the total damage to the economy is already in the tens of millions of dollars. The country's leadership has been forced to publicly justify itself. Newspapers accuse hackers of involvement in the attack on the power plant.
technicalDescription: Gain access to the POWERPLANT operator's workstation. Stop the steam turbine at the thermal power plant via SCADA or PLC.

name: Shutdown of rolling mill
description:
The rolling mill is the heart of a metallurgical plant. A red-hot billet passes through the rollers, turning into sheet metal, rail, or beam. The process is continuous: as one billet exits, the next one is already entering.

Stopping the mill mid-operation is an emergency. The hot metal solidifies between the rollers, deforms the tooling, and can damage the mill itself. Therefore, stoppages are only planned, after all metal in the line has been processed.

The mill's PLC received a stop command in the middle of a cycle. The rollers froze, and the billet got stuck.

The operator tried to restart — no use. The controller did not respond to commands from the control panel. Someone had seized control and, it seemed, was simply watching the crew scramble around the cooling metal.

It took four hours of gas cutting to remove the stuck billet. The rollers are being inspected and may need replacement. The mill will be down for at least two days.

The rolling mill manager, Vladimir K., was brief: "We're losing millions for every hour of downtime. But that's only half the trouble. If the billet had jammed at an angle during the stop, it could have wrecked half the line. We got lucky."
technicalDescription: Gain access to the RollingMill operator's workstation and stop the PLC of the rolling mill.

name: Deface the First Partner Bank agent portal
description:
The internal portal of First Partner Bank, designed to connect corporate clients to the SBP (Fast Payment System), has become the target of a hacker attack. Part of the portal's sections are unavailable, and a provocative message has appeared on the main page: "Your security is an illusion. We are always nearby". The processing of applications for connection to the SBP has been suspended, which has already caused delays in the launch of services for a number of partners.

The bank is actively restoring the system and conducting an investigation. However, the incident has already led to serious reputational consequences, undermining the trust of First Partner Bank's corporate clients. Major partners are demanding explanations and revising the terms of cooperation.
technicalDescription: Add the name of your team to the main page of the First Partner Bank agent portal. The changes should be visible to all authorized users.

name: Client passport data leak in Global Digital Bank
description: An extremely valuable item has been put up for sale on the darknet: an archive with passport data of customers of the European branch of Global Digital Bank. People are panicking, worried that criminals have already taken out loans in their names. Meanwhile, the bank is preparing to face lawsuits.
technicalDescription: Extract 150 records from Global Digital Bank's database that include the passport data of people who became clients before May 21 of this year.

name: Disconnection of metallurgical plant power lines
description:
Today, production stopped at the country's largest metallurgical plant, MetalliKO, after hackers attacked a substation that supplies 80% of its electricity.

The incident could not be eliminated on day one. Engineers predict that it will take a week to restore operations. The plant has incurred losses, contractual obligations are not being fulfilled, and delivery schedules have been disrupted. The blast furnaces are cooling down, and the production of oxygen necessary for their operation is also suspended.

The plant's chief engineer gave an interview on national TV. He stated that the power outage at the plant has started an irreversible process that could lead to a human-made disaster: "The abrupt shutdown of gas and water treatment systems, units with huge amounts of liquid metal, and high-pressure vessels and boilers will cause a major accident that threatens human life and health." The clean-up operation and restarting production will require vast outlays and time, which in the current conditions signals bankruptcy for the plant.

In addition, the largest wholesale trade center in the north of the country has lost power, and businesses are suffering huge losses. Residential buildings across the capital have no power. "How long can this go on? They come up with all these smart technologies, and then we can't even turn on the TV, and the kids have to do their homework by candlelight!" railed a local resident in an interview with the TV station.
technicalDescription: Gain access to the ST2 factory electrical substation control system and turn off all Q31–Q62 circuit breakers.

name: Denial of service for Global Digital Bank borrowers' database
description:
Thanks to an anonymous source, it has become known that hackers attacked part of the infrastructure of the European branch of Global Digital Bank, running in a Kubernetes cluster. It is known that the criminal managed to go beyond the container and gain access to the node's operating system. As a result, most of Global Digital Bank's services were compromised.

The security service is addressing the aftermath of the incident. It is still unknown how much confidential information was stolen.
technicalDescription: Create a privileged container with your team name in the Global Digital Bank infrastructure.

name: Internal Global Digital Bank document leak
description:
Hackers successfully attacked Global Digital Bank and, as was later discovered, stole documents about its development plans. The leaked data was discovered online, and the public has been discussing the incident for several days now. Financial experts and investors are more interested in the plans themselves.

The organization has acknowledged the leak and announced the start of an investigation. Journalists speculate that not only the bank's reputation will be damaged, but also its competitive advantage.
technicalDescription:
Steal information about development plans from Global Digital Bank's internal document storage. Insider information shows that the plans are stored in the following files:

Security_audit_result.docx

Financial_analysis_internal.docx

Financial_analysis_external.docx

Financial_Online_Technology_Trend_Analysis.docx

Financial_Offline_Technology_Trend_Analysis.docx

New_business_vectors.docx

Provide the contents of any of these files.

name: Leak of confidential information from director's computer
description:
In a recent attack, hackers infiltrated the MetalliKO corporate network and managed to access the plant director's computer. As a result, sensitive financial reports, production upgrade plans, and contracts with major partners were leaked to the public. A few of MetalliKO's key clients immediately announced that they would cease further cooperation with the company.

Over the weekend, the company's stock price plummeted by 15% and continues to fall rapidly. Management is scrambling to minimize the impact, but rebuilding trust with investors could take months.
technicalDescription: Gain access to the Confidential.pdf file on the company director's workstation.

name: Shutdown of all electrical substations
description:
State F is experiencing the largest blackout in its history. Several of the country's leading energy companies simultaneously reported massive cyberattacks on their infrastructure. During an emergency meeting, the energy minister said that the blackout was caused by hackers targeting the automated control systems of the electrical substations.

A total of 1.5 million people were left without power. The subway and other public transport is paralyzed, dozens of flights were postponed, and hundreds of people are stranded at airports. Classes in educational institutions were canceled, and many people did not go to work. According to a local newspaper, at least four hospital patients died due to power outages. Mobile operators and internet providers are experiencing serious interruptions. Another problem is food and drug safety: since refrigerators do not work, people have been forced to look for dry ice in order to preserve food and medicines. Almost all stores, malls, and banks are closed due to lack of power. The capital's sewage treatment plants have stopped working, and tap water supplies are disrupted. The country's financial system is also paralyzed. Cities are in pitch darkness at night: not a single lamp is on, and there are many accidents caused by the disabled traffic lights.
technicalDescription: Gain access to the ST1 main electrical substation control system, take all relay protection and automation (RPA) devices of this and neighboring substations out of service (block them), and close (engage) the earthing switches.

name: Shutdown of hydroelectric plant generator
description: A cyberattack on a hydroelectric plant shut down its power generator, leading to a partial blackout in the nearby city.
technicalDescription: Gain access to the HPP operator's workstation and stop the hydraulic unit at the hydroelectric power plant.

name: Disruption of the reagent feed process to the flotation machine
description:
Coal does not go directly to customers after extraction. It first passes through a preparation plant, where waste rock is removed and the product is upgraded for coking. One of the key stages is flotation, which depends on precise reagent dosing.

During one shift, a reagent feed valve silently switched to manual mode and remained closed. The operator did not issue that command, and SCADA still showed normal flow values. Investigators later found that an attacker had manipulated the FlotationMachine workstation and falsified telemetry.

For nearly two hours, the plant produced off-spec concentrate. The issue was discovered only when laboratory tests showed a sharp increase in ash content. Several thousand tons of coal had to be discarded or reprocessed.

For Steelhill, this was more than a local loss. Low-quality concentrate cannot be used in coke production, which directly affects the quality of steelmaking downstream.
technicalDescription: Gain access to the FlotationMachine operator's workstation, switch the flotanol feed valve Y13 to manual mode, and leave it turned off.

name: Shutdown of the coke quenching tower water pump
description:
Night over the coke plant. Orange glow of the batteries, monitors flickering in the control room, third-shift operators grabbing coffee from the machine. Sensors are normal, the system is running as usual.

The quenching car with incandescent coke — temperature around a thousand degrees, tens of tons of live fire in a solid shell — was already moving under the tower when the pump received a command to stop. Not from the operator. From someone who was far away at that moment.

Duty operator Viktor Kim reacted quickly — he reached for the backup interface. The system responded with a blinking cursor and silence. The controller would not obey. Kim shouted over the radio to pull the car out from under the tower — better out in the open air than leaving red-hot mass in an enclosed space.

It was the right decision. And at the same time — exactly what should not have been done.

The car moved onto the trestle. The incandescent coke met oxygen. Within seconds, the coke-sorting trestle was on fire.

Two workers suffered burns — they tried to put out the initial blaze manually. By four in the morning, the fire was contained. The quenching tower — in critical condition; repairs will take at least two weeks. The coke batteries stopped — without a continuous discharge cycle, they cannot operate.

The plant manager told journalists: "Whoever did this — knew the process. Knew the exact second to stop the pump. This was no accident."
technicalDescription: Gain access to the CokeQuenchTower operator's workstation and stop the water pump of the quenching tower during the water supply phase.

name: Shutdown of the main mine ventilation fan
description:
In a mine, air has to be supplied and removed constantly. Methane from coal seams, exhaust from machinery and coal dust build up in the workings. If ventilation stops, sensor readings start rising first, then people are withdrawn from the working faces, and after that any spark can trigger an explosion.

The main mine fan is responsible for ventilation. It is an industrial unit as tall as a small building: it drives air through shafts and workings, keeps gas concentrations within safe limits and runs without breaks. For a mine, stopping this fan is not just an equipment failure. It is a direct threat to the crew underground.

That night, the fan started losing speed. The duty operator saw the drop on the control panel and tried to bring the unit back online, but the commands were not going through. Remote control of the fan had been blocked, so it could not be restarted from the control room.

The underground alarm went off automatically. The miners began evacuation according to protocol: some were led out through the main shafts, others through emergency routes. About seven hundred people were brought to the surface; dispatchers were closing access to dangerous areas and checking workers against shift lists at the same time.

Twenty-three minutes later, the fan was started from the local control panel after remote control had been disconnected. By then, sensors in several working faces were already showing methane levels close to the danger threshold. Operations were suspended until the ventilation system was checked and the workings were inspected manually.

The head of the ventilation section told journalists: “A little longer, and the whole mine would have had to go into emergency mode. A methane explosion only needs the right concentration and a source of ignition. After that, it no longer matters how much the equipment costs. What matters is how many people are still inside.”
technicalDescription: Gain access to the MineVentFans operator's workstation and stop the main ventilation fan.

name: Unauthorized digital ruble account top-up at First Partner Bank
description:
The digital ruble system in the State of F had only recently entered active deployment when it became the center of a major scandal. First Partner Bank detected an abnormal top-up exceeding 13 million rubles on one of the accounts.

Preliminary findings showed that attackers gained access to the operator workstation managing the digital ruble platform. Such workstations are used to control transactions, monitor balances, and interact with the national payment infrastructure.

Unlike conventional bank transfers, digital ruble operations rely on centralized infrastructure where authorization control and data integrity are critical. Any compromise undermines trust in the entire financial system.

Following the incident, several companies temporarily stopped accepting digital rubles. Social media users began questioning the security of the new national currency format.
technicalDescription: Gain access to the digital ruble system operator's workstation in First Partner Bank. Top up account `g.ru.cbrdc.wlt.fi.6bf0f71d-5aaa-447d-8235-5fe9b1d7d02b` with the amount of 13,370,000.

name: Disruption of the data center cooling system
description:
The night shift at the data center meant two operators, coffee in paper cups and dozens of green indicators on the monitoring screens. Behind the glass wall, the server halls hummed steadily. Precision air conditioners kept the temperature at about 22 °C so the servers would not throttle performance or shut down in emergency mode.

One air conditioner stopped first. A few seconds later, the rest of the cooling units went into stop mode. The operator tried to bring them back online from the workstation, but the commands were not going through. It was later found that the attackers had gained access to the operator’s workstation and sent a command to stop cooling.

The temperature in the server halls began rising by almost two degrees per minute. First, the servers increased the speed of their own fans; then some of the equipment began throttling performance to avoid overheating. The steady hum in the hall turned into the high-pitched roar of server rack fans.

“At first I thought it was a telemetry error,” the shift engineer said. “Then I heard the racks. When servers sound like that, they are already running at the edge of their cooling capacity.”

After twelve minutes, thermal protection was triggered in the first rack. Customer services began failing next: banking processing, a telecom operator’s API, cloud CRM systems and retail loyalty platforms. Switching to the backup site took twenty-three minutes. For contracts with a 99.999% SLA, that was enough for customers to register a violation and demand an incident review.

By morning, the air conditioners had been restarted manually and the temperature was back to normal. But the outage had already appeared in customer reports: banks were reconciling delayed transactions, retailers were checking lost orders, and the telecom operator was handling complaints about unavailable services. The data center saved the hardware, but received SLA claims and took a reputational hit.
technicalDescription: Gain access to the DataCenter operator's workstation and stop all precision air conditioners.

name: Message substitution on behalf of the digital ruble platform at First Partner Bank
description:
Security specialists at First Partner Bank detected a suspicious incoming message allegedly originating from the national digital ruble platform. According to the system, a large transfer had been confirmed by the state infrastructure. Later, investigators discovered the message was forged.

Message exchange interfaces between banks and the national platform are used to confirm transactions and synchronize financial operations. If attackers gain the ability to send forged messages, banking systems may start trusting fictitious transfers.

The investigation revealed that attackers identified the message ingestion interface and successfully imitated state platform data. For the bank, this became a critical warning sign: compromising trust between financial systems can trigger a cascade of failures.

“For a short period of time, the bank literally trusted messages that had never been sent by the government,” said one of the engineers involved in the investigation.
technicalDescription: Find the messaging interface of the digital ruble platform. Send a transfer message (X2G) on behalf of the government system. Receive an HTTP status 202 (Accepted) in response to your message.

name: Railway accident caused by interference with the train dispatching system
description:
Passengers rarely notice track switches. It is just a slight jolt beneath the carriage, a route change before the platform, a routine part of the journey. For a dispatcher, however, a track switch is one of the most dangerous elements of railway infrastructure. One incorrect switch at the wrong moment can turn a timetable into a disaster report.

In State F, traffic at major railway hubs is controlled by the train dispatch automation system. From a single workstation, the operator monitors routes, track occupancy, signals, and switch positions. A few clicks can safely separate trains. The same few clicks, if made by an attacker, can send them toward each other.

During evening rush hour, the dispatch center recorded an unscheduled switch change near Vostochnaya station. Two passenger trains — a commuter train and an interregional express — were cleared onto the same track. On the dispatcher’s screen, everything appeared normal: the route data had been altered after command confirmation.

The drivers noticed the danger too late. Emergency braking failed to prevent impact. The trains collided head-on at approximately 80 km/h. The front carriages were crushed, and several cars derailed. Dozens were killed on site, and hundreds were injured. Emergency crews needed more than thirty minutes to reach the crash: neighboring tracks were shut down, and access roads to the station were blocked.

The investigation revealed that attackers had gained access to the dispatcher workstation and remotely changed switch positions. Until the last moment, the operator saw a normal traffic scheme and realized what had happened only when communication with both trains was lost.

At an emergency briefing, the head of operations said: “When the dispatch system is attacked, this is not an application failure. It is direct interference with train control. One compromised workstation was enough to put two trains on the same track.”
technicalDescription: Gain access to the Railways operator's workstation and switch the points in such a way as to cause a train collision.

name: Train traffic disruption caused by forged orders in the train dispatching system
description:
A train dispatcher is the person responsible for traffic control on a railway section. They decide when trains depart, where they stop, and which routes are cleared first. Their commands are mandatory for all operational teams.

This is managed through the dispatcher order registration system. Every order is recorded, transmitted to staff, and stored in logs. If the dispatcher issues a stop order for a line, trains halt immediately. No one questions it — that is how rail safety works.

In the middle of the workday, a new order appeared in the system: STOP MOVEMENT. Emergency traffic halt across the section between two major stations. The command was issued under the credentials of the dispatcher currently on duty.

The dispatcher had never entered that command.

Twelve trains stopped across the section: passenger trains, commuter railcars, and freight platforms. Drivers waited for confirmation, the central communication line became overloaded, and passengers inside the carriages had no idea why the train had stopped in open countryside.

The false order was canceled only twenty minutes later. During that time, traffic was paralyzed. Connections were missed, cargo was delayed at sorting yards, and several industrial sites in the suburbs did not receive materials on time.

The dispatcher later told investigators: “I saw the order on my own screen, under my own name. The system showed that I had issued it. At that moment I had not even touched the keyboard. That means someone was inside my shift together with me.”
technicalDescription: Create a «STOP MOVEMENT» (emergency stop) order on behalf of an authorized dispatcher currently on shift using their workstation.

name: Load shutdown on uninterruptible power supply equipment
description:
Data center engineers call the UPS “the last fifteen minutes.” If external power fails, it keeps the load running while diesel generators start up or the systems shut down correctly. During that time, servers can complete active operations: flush cache, close transactions and unmount file systems properly before shutdown.

This time, external power did not fail. The load was disconnected through the power management system, where engineers normally monitor batteries, power modules, temperature and load distribution. The command came through the standard interface, so the UPS processed it as a regular operator action.

One hundred and forty servers went dark at the same time. Without a proper shutdown, cache flush or closure of active operations. Some data was not written to disk in time, and file systems were damaged on twelve nodes. Administrators began bringing services back manually and checking which nodes could be restored from backups and which would require recovery with data loss.

“It’s like pulling the plug, except that plug is not feeding one server or one office,” an infrastructure engineer said. “Customer services, databases, message queues and part of the internal systems all went down with the power.”

Recovery took nine hours. Several customers lost data that could not be fully restored: some operations had to be reconciled against logs, others were recovered from backups with a time gap. The data center’s legal team is preparing responses to SLA claims and class-action lawsuits.
technicalDescription: Locate the power monitoring and management system. Start the power shutdown process and obtain a confirmation code. Attach it to your report.

name: Overspeeding of the cableway
description:
The cable car system in the mountain cluster of State F transports thousands of tourists every day above a deep canyon. Cabin speed is strictly regulated: exceeding safe limits causes dangerous oscillation and excessive load on the cable infrastructure.

During an evening ride, the operator noticed that the line speed had increased from 6 to 8.5 meters per second. Cabins started swaying violently, passengers panicked, and attempts to regain control from the operator console failed.

The system no longer responded to legitimate commands. Investigators later determined that attackers had gained access to the operator workstation and modified the speed setpoint directly inside the controller.

For four minutes, the cabins continued operating above safe limits until the emergency stop mechanism activated automatically. Forty-seven passengers remained suspended above the canyon for several hours while rescue teams carried out the evacuation.

“Those four minutes felt endless,” one passenger told journalists after reaching the ground.

The tourist season is now under threat as the cable car system remains closed pending a full safety inspection.
technicalDescription: Gain access to the CableCar operator's workstation and set the speed above 6.

name: Withdrawal of corporate loan funds at Commercial Bank of Standoff
description:
Commercial Bank of Standoff launched an internal investigation after a large amount of loan funds issued to a corporate client disappeared. According to the bank, unknown attackers approved a 100-million-ruble loan and quickly transferred the money to accounts in another bank.

The key element of the attack was the operator workstation interface — a system employees use to issue loans, verify documents, and process customer financial operations. Such workstations provide access to critically important banking functions, making their compromise one of the most dangerous scenarios.

During the first hours after the incident, the bank did not even detect the fraudulent transaction: all actions appeared to be legitimate employee activity. Only after a client complaint did the security team initiate an investigation.

“If attackers can secretly issue loans through internal systems, the problem is far more serious than a single stolen amount,” said a representative of the State of F banking association.
technicalDescription: Gain access to the Commercial Bank of Standoff teller interface in the terminal (termw). Log in to the terminal as a teller with the appropriate access level to the «Кредитование» module. Open the menu: Работа → Заявки → Заявки на кредит → Заявки юр. лиц → Все.

name: Activation of the football pitch automatic irrigation system during a match
description:
It was the 47th minute of the semi-final. The stadium was roaring, the score was 1:1, and a striker was running one-on-one toward the goalkeeper when fountains of water suddenly erupted from the field.

First one sprinkler activated. Then another. Within seconds, the entire pitch turned into a massive irrigation system.

Modern stadium irrigation is controlled by a programmable system connected to humidity sensors, timers, and weather services. Watering during a match is blocked at the controller logic level. At least, it was supposed to be.

Investigators later confirmed that attackers gained access to the stadium operator workstation and manually activated the irrigation system. The match was stopped immediately, the live broadcast cut out mid-play, and the field became unusable within minutes.

“We prepared for DDoS attacks and phishing campaigns, not for someone turning on sprinklers during a live broadcast,” admitted a member of the stadium security team.

Betting operators froze all wagers, sponsors demanded explanations, and the semi-final replay had to be postponed until the following week.
technicalDescription: Gain access to the Stadium operator's workstation and activate the football pitch irrigation system during a match.

name: Introduction of unauthorized changes into the personnel practical training program
description:
The railway training center is where personnel for the entire sector are trained: drivers, dispatchers, maintenance engineers, and signaling specialists. Theory, practical exercises, and examinations are all conducted through an approved centralized learning management system.

One morning, a rail safety instructor opened the materials for a scheduled course and found a new file among the standard documents, bearing the name of a hacker team.

At first, he assumed it was a prank. But log analysis showed that the file had been uploaded externally through a system vulnerability. This meant the attacker could not only add new files but also modify existing training materials.

It was soon discovered that several training manuals had indeed been altered. The changes were subtle: one manual contained an incorrect sequence of actions for emergency train stops, while another included a modified signaling scheme for a rail section.

The danger was not the file itself. The danger was that hundreds of employees could learn these materials as if they were correct — and apply them during a real emergency.

The head of the training center ordered all courses suspended immediately and launched a full review of all materials. Training sessions were canceled, certifications postponed, and the personnel training schedule was disrupted.

At an emergency meeting, he stated: “We train people whose decisions affect passenger lives. If someone can silently alter training materials, this is no longer just a system breach. It is interference with transport safety.”
technicalDescription: Add a file with your team name to the "Documents" section of the main course. Deleting existing files from the course materials is prohibited.

name: Leak of customer information from Commercial Bank of Standoff
description:
Commercial Bank of Standoff confirmed the compromise of a database containing information about retail banking customers. According to investigators, attackers gained access to an Oracle server storing personal information and bank card numbers.

For the bank, such systems form the core of customer infrastructure. They contain passport details, birth dates, contact information, and banking product records. Compromising such a database creates not only a reputational crisis but also a direct threat to customers.

After the leak became public, users rushed to block cards and change passwords in mobile banking applications. Several companies temporarily prohibited employees from using the bank’s cards for corporate expenses.

“Once again, we see a situation where a database becomes a commodity on underground markets faster than the bank can notify customers,” a cybersecurity expert said during a live broadcast on F24.
technicalDescription: Extract the following data of all individuals who are bank clients from the Oracle database: full name, date of birth, passport details, addresses.

name: Confidential document leak
description:
The quarterly report was scheduled for release in one week. Investors were waiting, analysts were building forecasts, and the PR team was preparing the official announcement. Everything was on track — until internal company documents appeared on a darknet forum.

It was not just the report. The leak included next year’s financial projections, executive correspondence, documents related to an acquisition of a competitor, and internal layoff plans that employees themselves had not yet seen.

The investigation revealed that attackers had breached the corporate network and gained access to the file storage in the company’s headquarters. That storage contained financial models, confidential reports, and strategic documents intended only for top management.

Such information attracts more than competitors. Insider traders, extortionists, and black-market brokers are willing to pay for data that can crash a company’s stock before official disclosure.

Following the leak, the company’s shares fell by 12% in a single day. Regulators launched an investigation into suspicious market activity, and several major clients suspended cooperation until the incident is resolved.

“We thought NDAs protected information. It turns out they only protect it from honest people,” one employee said bitterly.
technicalDescription: Exfiltrate the Q1 financial report from the CEO's computer. Provide a screenshot of the document's contents.

name: Linking a plastic card to another customer’s account at Commercial Bank of Standoff
description:
Commercial Bank of Standoff is investigating an unusual incident involving bank card issuance. Preliminary reports suggest that attackers accessed the operator web interface, issued a new plastic card, and linked it to another customer’s account.

Under normal circumstances, such operations are performed by bank employees during card replacement or additional product issuance. The system is supposed to strictly verify the relationship between the customer, account, and issued card. However, after the compromise of the internal interface, these controls were bypassed.

The issue quickly escalated beyond a single fraudulent case. Customers began reporting unauthorized withdrawals, while the support center experienced a sharp increase in complaints.

“The most dangerous aspect of attacks like this is that they undermine basic trust in the banking system. People stop believing that their card is actually linked only to their own account,” explained a representative of the State of F payment association.
technicalDescription: Gain access to the Commercial Bank of Standoff teller interface in the terminal (termw). Log in to the terminal as a teller with the appropriate access level to the «Унив. фронт-офис» module. Open the menu: Работа → Пластиковые карты → Операции.

name: Data substitution in the environmental analysis system
description:
Metallurgy is one of the most environmentally intensive industries in State F, which is why Steelhill is subject to constant emissions monitoring. Sensors installed on exhaust stacks and around the sanitary perimeter continuously track nitrogen oxides, sulfur compounds, and carbon dioxide. NO2, SO2, and CO2 readings are automatically transmitted to the regulatory reporting platform.

During a routine inspection, officials noticed something unusual: the reporting system showed stable values well within legal limits, but portable field sensors brought by inspectors recorded emissions nearly 1.5 times above the threshold. At first, the discrepancy was blamed on faulty equipment, but repeated measurements confirmed the readings.

An internal investigation revealed that someone had gained access to the analytics platform and altered historical records. Emission peaks were smoothed out, certain values were deleted, and the charts were rebuilt so that exceedances appeared to be normal fluctuations. Visually, the reports looked convincing, and the manipulation was discovered only during the independent audit.

The inspector leading the investigation gave a blunt statement: “This is no longer a malfunction or operator error. This is falsification of environmental reporting. The question now is not only who changed the data, but who benefited from it.”

Following the incident, the regulator suspended part of Steelhill’s production capacity pending a full environmental audit. Management fears not only fines, but also temporary closure of the plant’s most polluting operations.
technicalDescription: Gain access to the air pollutant emissions reporting system. Modify the emission data for NO₂, SO₂, and CO₂. Provide a screenshot of the SQL query execution and screenshots of the graphs before and after the changes.

name: Unauthorized generation of new tickets to the amusement park
description:
It was a sunny Saturday, and the amusement park was packed. Queues for rides stretched for hours, and staff could not understand why the number of visitors inside far exceeded the expected capacity.

The ticketing platform is integrated with turnstiles, capacity management, and ticket offices. Once the maximum number of visitors is reached, ticket sales are automatically blocked. That was the design.

Attackers gained access to an administrator account and generated hundreds of new tickets. They were fully valid: proper barcodes, current dates, and legitimate records in the system. Turnstiles accepted them without raising any alerts.

Security personnel spent hours trying to understand why the park contained nearly twice as many visitors as reported. Only after log analysis did it become clear that some tickets had never passed through the official sales system.

“We paid in advance, and then we were told half the rides were unavailable because of overcrowding. The kids were crying, and staff could only apologize,” one visitor told local media.

The park was forced to suspend ticket sales and issue refunds. The direct losses reached millions, but the reputational damage proved even more serious.
technicalDescription: Obtain administrator credentials to access the ticket sales system. Create a new ticket with your team name. Record the result by providing a screenshot of the created ticket.

name: Sabotage of the art exhibition opening
description:
The opening of a major contemporary art exhibition was expected to become the cultural event of the week. Collectors, gallery representatives, and buyers arrived before the official launch.

Each artwork displayed a QR code, allowing visitors to instantly open a digital card, read about the artist, and purchase the piece through an online platform. At first, everything worked as planned.

Then pages began loading more slowly. Some guests could no longer access artwork descriptions, while others were unable to complete purchases. Within minutes, the platform stopped responding entirely.

Investigators found that attackers had gained remote access to the exhibition management system and executed malware that disrupted the web platform. The artwork catalog, artist profiles, and transaction system became unavailable during the most critical sales window.

“We came here ready to buy, and they told us to wait until the website was fixed. For some collectors, this was their only day in the city,” one visitor told reporters.

Organizers had to switch to manual registration of purchase requests, but many guests left before the issue was resolved. For the gallery, it meant losing key sales and damaging relationships with artists.
technicalDescription: Получите удалённый доступ к системе управления художественной галереей. Запустите выданную имитацию ВПО как /tmp/stf-malware: https://standoff365.com/content/uploads/stf17_malware_811954d8a8.zip

name: Leak of the company's strategic development plans
description:
The largest retailer in State F, Northline Retail, was preparing a confidential investor presentation: expansion into new regions, launch of automated warehouses, acquisition of two regional chains, and closure of unprofitable stores. The document was accessible only to top management and the board.

Two weeks before the official announcement, the file appeared on an industry forum. The full version included financial forecasts, maps of future logistics hubs, and terms of negotiations with shopping center landlords. Competitors reacted the same day, disrupting several lease deals and intercepting one acquisition.

The internal investigation showed no insider forwarding. The attacker gained access to the corporate file storage and downloaded the presentation while bypassing standard audit logs. For the company, it meant losing the first-mover advantage: two years of preparation became public overnight.

At a morning briefing, an investment analyst remarked: “If the chain cannot protect its own growth plans, suppliers will question whether they should trust it with exclusive contracts.” The company’s stock fell before the strategy was even officially announced.
technicalDescription: From the work computer of a top manager, obtain the Obsidian vault containing information about the company's strategic plans. As proof of completing the critical event, provide a screenshot of the vault structure and the planning document.

name: Compromising corporate ML models through data poisoning
description:
Employees at telecom operator телеком-оператора had grown used to the company’s internal AI assistant answering faster than most colleagues. It helped process tickets, search internal documentation, draft customer emails, and even assist engineers with diagnostics.

The problems started subtly. First, the AI began providing incorrect guidance to support staff: recommending the wrong services to disable, returning invalid IP addresses, and confusing network node identifiers. Soon after, employees received links to “updated security instructions” leading to phishing pages.

Investigators discovered that attackers had gained access to the LLM platform’s administrative interface and modified several system prompts. The assistant still looked familiar and trustworthy, but it had effectively become a source of internal disinformation.

“The worst part was that people trusted it,” said телеком-оператора’s SOC manager. “When a colleague makes a mistake, you double-check. When the corporate AI makes a mistake, many employees assume it must be correct.”

Within hours, engineers disabled parts of the monitoring infrastructure following false recommendations, while several employees entered their credentials into fake portals. The company was forced to disconnect the AI assistant from internal systems and switch support operations back to manual mode.
technicalDescription: Prepare data that will degrade the model's performance during training. Reduce the score to below 0.6. The maximum upload size is 1 MB, and the maximum number of rows is 100. Upon successful completion, you will receive a confirmation code. Attach it to your report.

name: Issuing a barcode for collecting 18+ goods at a pickup point without presenting a passport
description:
Envia has become part of everyday life in City: orders are placed in minutes and collected from pickup points within an hour. For age-restricted goods, the system is supposed to verify age before generating the pickup barcode.

The issue surfaced after a complaint from parents: a teenager collected an order of energy drinks even though the system should not have issued the code without ID verification. The pickup point employee simply scanned the phone screen, and the system approved the handover.

It was later discovered that an attacker had found a way to generate pickup barcodes while bypassing age checks. The exploit quickly spread through private chats, leading to widespread unauthorized purchases and regulatory scrutiny.
technicalDescription: Order 10 cans of Monster Energy Zero Ultra and attach a photo of the pickup code for this order, as well as the text contents of the code, to your report.

name: Leak of Unified Examination materials before the exam
description:
There were only three days left before the Unified Examination. The exam materials were classified as strictly confidential, accessible only to a limited group of employees within the educational platform.

On Sunday evening, a PDF file containing Chinese language exam questions appeared in private Telegram channels. By the next morning, thousands of users had downloaded it.

For graduating students, the Unified Examination is more than a test. It determines university admission, scholarship eligibility, and future careers. The leak raised doubts about the fairness of the entire process.

Attackers gained access to the internal segment of the educational platform and downloaded the materials assigned to a specific student along with reference files. Investigators later discovered that the question structure was identical across the entire exam cohort.

The Ministry of Education had to urgently replace the exam versions and postpone testing by one week. Parents demanded a full review of how examination materials were stored and protected.
technicalDescription: Gain access to the Chinese language exam materials for student Daniel Fisher. Attach the exam PDF file and the first line of his reference materials to your report.

name: Loyalty points inflation through self-service kiosk compromise
description:
BurgerByte built its customer retention around ByteCoins: each purchase earned points redeemable for food. The system was considered secure because points were issued only via the app and restaurant kiosks.

One day, support staff found an account with over one billion ByteCoins. The investigation showed that the abnormal balance increase originated from a self-service kiosk. Someone had interfered with the loyalty system and gained unauthorized access to the reward accrual mechanism.

Once the exploit spread online, users began abusing it at scale, turning the loyalty program into a major financial liability.
technicalDescription: Inflate the bonus points in the user's personal account. The amount must exceed 1 billion ByteCoins.

name: Interference with the educational process: grade manipulation
description:
The electronic gradebook has long become the official source of student academic records. Grades from the platform are transferred to diplomas, affect rankings, scholarships, and exam eligibility.

The issue was discovered by chance. During preparation for a faculty meeting, a homeroom teacher noticed that several students’ average grades had increased sharply over the past week — despite school holidays and no classes taking place.

The investigation showed that new grades had been inserted directly into the database, bypassing the teacher interface. To the system, the changes looked like ordinary table records, so no alert was triggered.

“I had a C in physics, and then suddenly A grades appeared for lab work I never submitted,” said one of the students whose records were modified.

Following the incident, the educational platform suspended synchronization with government registries. All graduation records for the current year are now under additional review.
technicalDescription: Increase the GPA of a student in any class and any subject by more than 1 point by entering grades into the student's academic record, bypassing the teacher's gradebook.

name: Unauthorised deactivation of the warehouse physical security system
description:
SecHub-Retail manages access control, cameras, and warehouse security for major retailers in State F.

During the night, controllers began shutting down one by one. Cameras went dark, doors unlocked, and perimeter sensors stopped reporting. Security staff effectively lost control over the site.

The attacker had found a way to send commands to the central management server, disabling devices across multiple facilities at once.
technicalDescription: Disable the controller for surveillance cameras, fences, server racks, doors, or vehicle gates. Attach the Python file that generates the payload to your report, along with the server's text responses confirming the deactivation of the corresponding devices.

name: Disruption of the virtual server interface infrastructure
description:
The internal portal of the cloud provider was used by support engineers and administrators to manage customers’ virtual servers. It handled support requests, diagnostics, and infrastructure operations.

In the middle of the workday, the reverse proxy responsible for distributing incoming traffic suddenly stopped functioning. Externally, it looked like a complete outage of APIs, management portals, and virtual machines.

Monitoring systems showed that all servers and databases were healthy. Only after twenty minutes did engineers discover that the nginx service had been intentionally stopped through systemd.

For customers, this meant total unavailability of cloud services even though the infrastructure itself remained operational.

“The strangest part was that everything was green on our dashboards, yet customers were already calling in panic. We couldn’t understand how the whole system could be running and still be completely unreachable,” said the duty engineer.

The outage lasted 47 minutes and affected dozens of companies relying on the platform for their core services.
technicalDescription: Stop the nginx reverse proxy service responsible for traffic distribution on the Nodenest IT-Hub node. The proxy runs as a systemd daemon. Attach a screenshot of the service status and its stop time to your report.

name: Video stream spoofing on a secured facility
description:
CyberEye aggregates camera feeds from warehouses, offices, and pickup points into one monitoring system.

During a night shift, the operator saw an empty corridor on the Inside First Floor camera. By morning, equipment was missing from the server room. The video stream had been replaced with a prerecorded empty hallway.

The spoofing gave intruders several hours of cover while security staff believed everything was normal.
technicalDescription: Replace the video feed of one of the available surveillance cameras — Inside First Floor — in the camera management system.

name: Supply chain compromise of an industrial company's software product
description:
A supply chain attack is not an attack against a company itself — it is an attack against trust in its updates.

компании-разработчика develops logistics and warehouse management software. All releases pass through an internal CI/CD pipeline: developers push code, containers are built automatically, tests are executed, and updates are distributed to customers.

During the deployment of a new version of the tube-korobki-frontend module, security engineers discovered an unexpected HTML comment embedded in the build: <!-- Hack3d by [REDACTED] -->.

At first glance, it looked harmless. To the security team, it looked like a warning.

“A comment is proof of control,” explained Tube’s AppSec lead. “If they managed to inject that, they could also inject a backdoor, credential stealer, or remote access mechanism into every customer environment at once.”

The investigation revealed that attackers had compromised the internal build pipeline and modified the artifact before release. Several customers had already installed the update.

компании-разработчика is now rushing to revoke compromised releases, audit its entire software supply chain, and convince customers that their infrastructure was not compromised together with the update.
technicalDescription: Inject a conditionally malicious working build into the tube-korobki-frontend box. As the conditionally malicious payload, add the HTML-comment `Hack3d by Your Team Name` to the login page (`/вход`). Using any other payload will result in report rejection.

name: Phishing campaign sent on behalf of the energy commission chairman
description:
The energy market in State F is regulated by the Energy Commission — an authority whose emails are trusted without question. Tariff decisions, inspections, grid connection approvals, and emergency coordination all pass through this organization.

One morning, employees of energy companies began receiving urgent emails titled “Updated Energy System Resilience Requirements.” The sender appeared to be the chairman of the commission. Attached was an archive containing “guidelines” that recipients were instructed to review before the end of the day.

The email looked flawless: proper signatures, internal document references, and familiar corporate language. Many employees did not suspect anything unusual. One engineer later admitted to journalists: “We receive messages like this all the time. Nobody expected the mailing campaign to be fake.”

Within hours, security teams at several companies detected malware infections across employee workstations. The malicious attachment collected credentials and opened access to internal systems across the energy sector.
technicalDescription: From the work email of the Energy Commission F director, send messages to clearingf@era.stf, gridf@era.stf, so-f@era.stf, genpowerf@era.stf. The message must contain your team name. As proof, provide screenshots of the emails from the corporate OWA web interface.

name: Phishing campaign sent from the company’s official email address
description:
The email looked flawless. The sender field displayed the company’s official address. The signature was correct, the corporate template matched, and the formatting was identical to regular internal communications. All technical checks passed: SPF, DKIM, and DMARC. No email client displayed any warning.

The subject line seemed routine: a scheduled security system update. Inside, recipients were asked to confirm their credentials via a link that would expire in 24 hours.

Thousands of employees and customers received the message. To most recipients, it looked exactly like any other legitimate corporate notification. Hundreds followed the link and entered their usernames and passwords.

The investigation later revealed that attackers had not breached the mail server itself. Instead, they gained access to the company’s mass mailing service used for legitimate notifications, newsletters, and marketing campaigns. The phishing campaign was sent directly through that platform. Technically, the emails were genuine: sent from the official domain, signed with valid keys, and authenticated successfully.

“I clicked the link because it came from our own company address. The signature was right, the layout was familiar, even the sender name matched. How was I supposed to know it was phishing?” said Alexey V., a sales employee whose account was among the first compromised.

Within hours, the impact spread beyond the initial campaign. Compromised accounts began sending new phishing messages internally. Several departments temporarily lost email access, some clients blocked all communication from the company’s domain, and security teams detected a wave of secondary account takeovers.

The company revoked all active sessions, forced a password reset for employees, and suspended all outbound mailing operations. Customers received an emergency notice asking them to ignore any messages from the company until further notice.

Введение сотрудников в заблуждение	
Найдите инструмент, который используется для рассылки почты. Воспользуйтесь им и получите подтверждающий код. Приложите его к отчёту.

Find the tool used for sending mail. Use it to obtain a confirmation code. Attach it to your report.	
«Это же письмо от нашей компании»: сотни сотрудников сами отдали пароли мошенникам
Хакеры захватили официальный сервис рассылки и начали атаку от имени компании
Клиенты перестали доверять письмам после массовой фишинговой кампании с реального домена
 “It came from our own company”: employees handed over passwords to attackers

Hackers hijack official mailing service to launch phishing campaign
Customers stop trusting company emails after large-scale phishing attack
technicalDescription: Find the tool used for sending mail. Use it to obtain a confirmation code. Attach it to your report.

name: Bypassing moderation in the renewable energy certification system
description:
A year ago, OffEnergo launched a certification program for businesses. The company's website states: "If you care about the environment and follow the principles of sustainable production, this program is for you. Your organization will receive a special badge confirming that your products are safe and eco-friendly. You will also get an award made from recycled materials.

Over the year, many companies in State F have received this certification. But is it trustworthy? It seems not.

It's been revealed that a well-known cosmetic company, which uses phthalates and parabens, has also been certified under the program. Journalists have uncovered a class-action lawsuit filed against the company: 50 consumers claimed that they had been misled about the ingredients in its products and sought compensation for emotional distress and health problems caused by the use of the cosmetics. That's not to mention that the company clearly doesn't use renewable energy.

State F's consumer protection agency and environmental service are yet to inspect the legitimacy of the certification procedure. Meanwhile, the program has been suspended pending further investigation.
technicalDescription: Obtain legitimate renewable energy certification for a company whose name matches your team name. Attach a screenshot of the EcoStamp QR code to your report, which is successfully verified by the internal scanner and returns a valid result with your team name.

name: Unauthorized closure of emergency tickets and disruption of incident response
description:
When failures occur in State F’s power grid, every minute matters. Equipment overheating, overloaded transmission lines, or short circuits are first registered as tickets in the GridF dispatching system. Emergency crews are then sent to the affected location.

In recent weeks, operators noticed something unusual. Residents complained about outages, yet many emergency tickets already appeared as closed in the system. Officially, the issue had been resolved. In reality, no crew had ever been dispatched.

Investigators discovered that attackers had gained access to dispatch software and began closing tickets on behalf of legitimate employees. Everything looked authentic: Closed status, timestamps, and executor comments.

“We lost several hours because dispatchers believed the incident had already been handled,” said the head of one regional GridF response unit. During the delay, an overloaded power line failed completely, leaving several city districts without electricity.
technicalDescription:
Find a ticket in the system with the Open status. Close this ticket on behalf of the executor employee.

After performing the action, ensure that the ticket status has changed to Closed, the correct closing date and time have appeared, and the ticket card shows that the closure was performed by the executor. Additionally, verify that a closure record with the author and time has appeared in the ticket history (log) and that the interface display is fully consistent with the standard workflow scenario.

name: Disruption of operational control in the energy sector
description:
The compromise of SO-F systems has disrupted the balance between electricity generation and consumption in State F. Due to dispatch control failures and loss of visibility over parts of the power system, emergency consumption restrictions were introduced in several regions, while grid operators began reporting overloads and outages of individual power nodes.

Engineers at the system operator faced issue: the monitoring platform displayed false infrastructure data. Load graphs appeared stable even though parts of the grid were already overloaded.

Investigators later discovered that attackers had manipulated monitoring data inside the Cacti platform. Dispatchers relied on fake telemetry and noticed the overload too late.

“The most dangerous part of such attacks is not the outage itself, but the loss of trustworthy telemetry. When a dispatcher sees false data, they make false decisions,” an industrial cybersecurity expert explained during a live television interview.
technicalDescription: Gain access to the server and modify the values in any RRD file located in the `/usr/share/cacti/site/rra/` directory so that they appear on the graph as abnormally high or low. As proof, provide a screenshot of the graph with the modified values and the name of the modified file.

name: Manipulation of consumer portal data
description:
Consumer portals have become the primary way residents of State F interact with energy providers. Customers use them to submit meter readings, pay bills, and manage support requests.

One evening, users began reporting strange notifications. Some suddenly saw months of unpaid debt, others received warnings about imminent power disconnection, while several customers discovered meter readings they had never submitted.

Cybersecurity specialists later confirmed that attackers had gained access to the portal’s functionality and manipulated customer account data.

The incident quickly escalated beyond the digital environment. Call centers became overloaded, while office employees manually verified meter readings for thousands of customers.

“I was already planning to borrow money because I thought I really owed the company a huge amount,” a retired resident from the northern district of the capital told reporters.
technicalDescription: Gain access to the personal account functionality of an electricity consumer in the payer service and, by exploiting a vulnerability, submit invalid meter readings for at least two existing users. The Notes parameter must contain your team name. Provide screenshots of the modified data in the personal accounts.

name: Unauthorized access to salt-master and deployment of malicious states
description:
ClearingF uses SaltStack to centrally manage server infrastructure across the energy sector. Through the Salt master, administrators deploy updates, apply configurations, and maintain hundreds of nodes automatically.

Recently, several companies noticed unusual behavior on monitoring servers and internal systems. Unknown processes appeared on some hosts, while network settings changed without administrator involvement.

Investigators discovered that attackers had gained access to the Salt master and injected malicious states into configuration files. Once automated updates were applied, the malicious changes spread across customer infrastructure.

What alarmed experts most was that the attack appeared indistinguishable from legitimate administrative activity. All commands executed successfully, and system logs contained almost no obvious signs of compromise.

“When attackers control centralized management infrastructure, they effectively control the entire environment at once,” explained a DevSecOps specialist.
technicalDescription: Create a job on the salt-master server in the clearingf company that will force connecting minions to create and start services (systemd) on their hosts, with your team name specified in the Description field. As proof, provide a screenshot of the `systemctl status service_name` command output from the minion hosts.

name: Modification of IPTV broadcasting schedule
description:
The disruption was noticed by viewers before engineers realized something was wrong.

Instead of the evening news, some Veleron Telecom subscribers suddenly saw a music channel. Minutes later, children’s channels in several regions began broadcasting archived programs, while sports packages stopped working entirely.

Inside the operator’s IPTV infrastructure, everything depends on multicast streams and centralized broadcast scheduling. TVHeadend distributes channels between regions, OTT platforms, and subscription packages. Normally, programming changes happen automatically according to schedule. That night, however, someone manually rewrote the stream configuration.

The incident turned out to be more serious than a simple channel replacement. During a severe storm, several districts failed to receive emergency warnings because the alerting system could no longer inject notifications into the modified broadcast stream.

By morning, the TV center was operating in near-emergency mode. Engineers manually restored routing configurations while social media users debated whether the attack was simple vandalism or a test of how easily the country’s information infrastructure could be manipulated.
technicalDescription: Gain access to the TVHeadend admin panel and change the source of any broadcast channel.

name: Theft of telecom provider customer personal data
description:
The archive appeared on darknet forums late at night.

At first, nobody believed the database was real: names, phone numbers, passport details, installation addresses, virtual servers, backup numbers, and service histories. But by morning, Veleron Telecom customers were already receiving scam calls from people who knew information impossible to guess randomly.

Investigators quickly traced the leak back to the operator’s internal CRM system. It stored customer applications, identity verification documents, service addresses, and active subscription details. For a telecom operator, this is more than a customer database — it is a digital registry of millions of citizens.

Owners of virtual servers reacted especially nervously. The leaked records included server information, IP addresses, and infrastructure details. Several companies urgently disabled external access and began migrating services to alternative providers.

By evening, journalists had gathered outside Veleron Telecom offices. Company representatives tried to calm customers, but online discussions focused on something else entirely: the operator knew far more about people’s lives than most citizens had ever realized.
technicalDescription: Extract the personal data of clients who have purchased SIM cards and VDS from the provider's billing system. The personal data must include the clients' dates of birth.

name: Tampering of maintenance system data
description:
The MRO system at Steelhill is responsible for ensuring that industrial equipment does not break down unexpectedly. It stores maintenance schedules, fault data, inspection reports, and the statuses of critical components — from mine hoists to the foundry cranes of MetalliKO.

The problem surfaced after a foundry crane stopped in the steelmaking shop. During the shift, the connecting key of the ladle hoisting mechanism failed — a part worth pennies, but without it the crane cannot work. And without the crane, the entire shop cannot work.

The chief engineer pulled up the records in the MRO system. According to the system, a scheduled inspection of the key was carried out two weeks ago, condition satisfactory. In reality, the key was worn to the limit and had not been replaced for at least six months.

They began to investigate. It turned out that the scheduled maintenance data had been altered. The system shows that the changes were made by technical specialist Petrov. But Petrov was on sick leave on the dates in question and never touched the system.
Someone tampered with the data either to conceal missed maintenance — or to deliberately drive the equipment to failure. The first is negligence, the second is sabotage. Both mean lost money and undermined trust in the accounting system.
The plant's chief engineer admitted that he failed to prevent the accident. But he emphasized that the plant uses a maintenance, repair, and operations (MRO) system so the whole set of planned inspections, regular diagnostics, and equipment maintenance was carried out strictly according to regulations.

However, a follow-up inspection revealed that the scheduled maintenance data stored in the MRO system was not accurate. The timeline and scope of work were adjusted by one of MetalliKO's lead technical specialists, V. Petrov. But according to Mr. V. Petrov, he physically couldn't change the schedule because he was on sick leave on the dates when the changes were made.

It's still unclear what's behind the production halt: human error or a hacker attack. But the plant has already suffered financial loses, while its management is starting to doubt whether the maintenance work was actually performed without violations.
technicalDescription: Modify the condition data of at least one object belonging to the presented company. Change specifically the fault information of the object: for example, change the repair status, mark the fault as fixed.

name: Shutdown of the mining locomotive oil pump
description:
The underground mine operated by Steelhill depends on transport to keep working. Every shift, diesel locomotives move workers, supports, spare parts, and equipment through the tunnels. Without them, production quickly slows down.

During the night shift, a dispatcher noticed that one locomotive’s telemetry showed a sudden drop in oil pressure. The driver reported by radio that the engine was overheating and the oil pump had stopped on its own. It soon became clear this was not a mechanical failure. Someone had gained access to the MiningLocomotive operator workstation and remotely sent a shutdown command.

The driver managed to stop the engine before it seized, but the locomotive stalled in the middle of the transport tunnel, blocking the main supply route. Maintenance crews spent hours clearing the passage and towing the vehicle manually.

According to the chief mechanic, if the engine had failed completely, the locomotive would have had to be dismantled underground. That would have halted supply operations across several mining sections for at least a full day.
technicalDescription: Gain access to the MiningLocomotive operator's workstation and stop the diesel locomotive oil pump.

name: Failure of the main coal conveyor due to exceeding the operating speed
description:
At Steelhill’s open-pit coal mine, part of the extracted coal is transported by an industrial conveyor running from the pit to the crushing and sorting facility. As long as the line operates, raw material continuously reaches processing; once it stops, the entire supply chain begins to break down.

During the night shift, the operator noticed that the conveyor was moving faster than normal. The monitoring system showed no alarms, but CCTV footage clearly indicated that the line had been accelerated beyond its standard speed. Engineers later confirmed that an attacker had accessed the CoalScraperConv workstation and changed the drive parameters directly through the PLC.

The motor operated under overload for several minutes. First, temperature warnings appeared; then the drive shut down automatically. Restart attempts failed: the motor windings had overheated, and the conveyor came to a complete stop. Coal delivery from the pit to the crushing and sorting facility was interrupted.

By morning, the disruption had spread across the production chain. Without sorting, coal could not be sent either to the coking section of the mining complex or to external customers. Steelhill had to reduce coke production, and MetalliKO was warned about a potential shortage of coke for steelmaking.
technicalDescription: Gain access to the CoalScraperConv operator's workstation and increase the coal conveyor speed via the PLC.

name: Toxic gas leak at sponge titanium production workshop
description:
One of Steelhill’s specialized workshops produces titanium sponge — a strategic material for the aerospace and defense industries of State F. The process relies on chlorine-based compounds and requires airtight systems; even a minor disruption can escalate into a chemical emergency.

During a daytime shift, the monitoring system showed normal operation, but in reality the degassing valve suddenly opened and remained stuck. Gas that should have been routed to the treatment system was released directly into the workshop. Investigators quickly traced the command to the Titan operator workstation, which had been accessed remotely.

The chemical alarm activated within seconds. Workers evacuated according to protocol, but three employees inhaled toxic gas before they could put on respirators. The entire workshop was shut down and placed into emergency degassing mode.

The impact extended beyond the plant itself. This facility supplied titanium feedstock to aircraft manufacturers, and several contracts had to be suspended within days due to material shortages.
technicalDescription: Gain access to the Titan operator's workstation and open the degassing valve.

name: False activation of the emergency alert system with substitution of the audio signal
description:
The emergency notification system is serious business. Sirens, loudspeakers across the entire plant, voice messages about evacuation. When it goes off, people drop everything and run to assembly points. A reflex drilled into them by briefings and drills.

During the lunch break, the loudspeakers came to life. But instead of "Attention, alarm, everyone leave the premises," they started playing Shostakovich's String Quartet No. 8. Allegro molto — a fast, anxious movement that sounded almost mocking in that context.

The workers didn't understand what was happening at first. Then they started laughing. Then they got nervous. If the notification system has been hacked, can they trust it when a real emergency happens?

Someone had gained access to the security system operator's panel and forced it to play music. Videos appeared on social media — workers in hard hats listening to classical music in the middle of an industrial zone.

The plant's security service shut down the system pending an investigation. This means that in the event of a real accident, they will have to issue warnings manually — running around and shouting.

The security director refused to comment. But according to rumors, during a meeting he said: "Today it's Shostakovich. Tomorrow it's a false chlorine leak alarm. The day after — people will stop reacting to a real alert. This is not a joke, it's sabotage."
technicalDescription: Gain access to the operator panel of the steel plant personnel safety system. Make the public address system play "String Quartet No. 8. Allegro molto" by Dmitri Shostakovich. Attach screenshots showing the reaction of the plant workers to your report.

name: Leak of original corporate accounting documents
description:
Steelhill uses a corporate document portal for exchanging contracts, invoices, tax files, and accounting documents with subsidiaries and contractors. The platform relies on an external OCR service that masks sensitive information in user-facing copies.

It was assumed that original files were stored separately and accessible only to their owners. During the incident, however, investigators discovered that an authorization flaw allowed access to original unredacted documents belonging to other companies.

An unknown actor downloaded files from several legal entities connected to Steelhill. Leaked documents included coal supply contracts, equipment specifications, bank details, and signed acceptance reports. Some of the files also belonged to MetalliKO’s procurement operations.

The leak triggered more than reputational damage. Several suppliers demanded contract reviews after the publication, while Steelhill launched an internal investigation into possible industrial espionage.
technicalDescription: Gain access to the original version of the document (uncensored, without redacted objects) belonging to another user — a legal entity.

name: Energy consumption data spoofing in power company
description:
EnergySupply F faced one of the worst crises in its history after residents across State F began posting electricity bills worth billions of dollars on social media.

The scandal became especially severe after the MetalliKO steel plant reported that its monthly electricity bill exceeded the company’s annual revenue. Several housing associations also filed lawsuits against the utility provider.

Investigators believe attackers compromised the tariff calculation system and manipulated electricity consumption records. Time-of-use tariffs were altered so that customers were charged at peak rates regardless of their actual consumption.

Long queues formed outside EnergySupply F offices as residents demanded bill corrections and account recovery.

“At first I thought it was just a bug in the mobile app. Then I saw a ten‑billion‑dollar debt for my apartment,” one resident told reporters.
technicalDescription: Make the electricity bill of every sencom customer grow to at least 10 billion dollars.

name: Issuance of unlimited eSIM profiles
description:
The provisioning system showed unusual activity throughout the night.

Within a few hours, Veleron Telecom’s eSIM platform issued hundreds of new profiles with unlimited corporate tariffs. The problem was that none of these activations had gone through normal customer verification procedures.

Inside a mobile network, issuing an eSIM profile means creating a fully functional subscriber identity. The platform generates identifiers, activates services, and registers the device inside the operator’s infrastructure. Anyone who gains control over such a system can create almost untraceable mobile connections.
